name: Montañas Vacías: La Toba Reservoir Loop
name_original: Beamud desde Uña — circular por el Embalse de la Toba
description: This difficult 30.0 miles (48.3 km) touring cycling route circles the Embalse de la Toba in Serranía de Cuenca, offering stunning views.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Laguna de Uña offers diverse landscapes within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. The region features a karst-origin lagoon with calm waters reflecting steep rocks and abundant vegetation. Surrounding areas include pine forests and unique geological formations such as the Torcas de los Palancares and Tierra Muerta. The terrain varies from mostly paved roads to some unpaved sections, with routes encompassing both gentle gradients and significant elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Laguna de Uña The most popular touring cycling route is Montañas Vacías: La Toba Reservoir Loop, a 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging ride around the Embalse de la Toba. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Enchanted City – Uña Viewpoint loop from Uña, a moderate 10.4 miles (16.8 km) path. This route combines natural beauty with notable attractions like the Enchanted City and the Uña Viewpoint. Local touring cyclists also love the View of the Laguna de Uña – Uña Lagoon loop from Uña, a 16.3 miles (26.2 km) trail leading through the scenic surroundings of the lagoon, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Laguna de Uña is defined by serene waters, dramatic geological formations, and rich pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes. The routes in Laguna de Uña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 1 review. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Laguna de Uña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Laguna de Uña?
A: The terrain around Laguna de Uña offers a mix of surfaces. You'll find both mostly paved roads and some unpaved sections. The routes vary from gentle gradients to significant elevation changes, providing diverse experiences within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park.

Q: Are there routes suitable for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Yes, Laguna de Uña has several challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084230269/montanas-vacias-la-toba-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías: La Toba Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 48.3 km ride with substantial elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40297360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Callejones de Las Majadas – Los Callejones de Las Majadas loop from Pozo Villar&lt;/a&gt;, which also features significant elevation changes.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling in the area?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You'll cycle past the serene waters of Laguna de Uña, surrounded by steep rocks and abundant vegetation. The area is home to diverse flora and fauna, including aquatic birds, otters, and trout. The surrounding forests are filled with pine trees, aromatic herbs like thyme and lavender, and unique geological formations such as the Torcas de los Palancares and Tierra Muerta.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling routes around Laguna de Uña?
A: While there are no routes specifically marked as 'easy' in the immediate vicinity, some moderate routes might be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with moderate distances and elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1095090297/los-callejones-de-las-majadas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Callejones de Las Majadas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 30.5 km route that could be an option for families looking for a longer ride.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I can visit along the cycling routes?
A: Several routes incorporate significant attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24537968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City – Uña Viewpoint loop from Uña&lt;/a&gt; combines natural beauty with the famous 'Enchanted City' (Ciudad Encantada) and the Mirador de la Laguna de Uña, offering breathtaking panoramic views. The area also features the picturesque village of Uña itself.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Laguna de Uña are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084230269/montanas-vacias-la-toba-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías: La Toba Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24537968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City – Uña Viewpoint loop from Uña&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Laguna de Uña – Uña Lagoon loop from Uña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Laguna de Uña?
A: The routes in Laguna de Uña are rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene waters to dramatic geological formations, and the variety of options for different ability levels.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Laguna de Uña area?
A: There are 8 touring cycling routes available around Laguna de Uña, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult, catering to different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Laguna de Uña itself?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Laguna de Uña – Uña Lagoon loop from Uña&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase the scenic surroundings of the lagoon, providing beautiful views of its calm waters and the reflecting landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Laguna de Uña?
A: The region offers diverse conditions throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring colder weather. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before planning your trip.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay in Uña village?
A: Yes, the charming village of Uña, located near the lagoon, offers options for visitors. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation facilities in the village, making it a convenient base for exploring the cycling routes and the wider Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while cycling?
A: The Laguna de Uña area is rich in biodiversity. While cycling, you might spot various aquatic birds such as moorhens, shoveler ducks, and cormorants. Otters and common trout are also present in the waters. The surrounding forests are home to diverse flora and fauna typical of the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park.
tips: Wonderful place that the town of Uña offers us in the heart of the Serranía de Cuenca. Very close to the urban area is the Laguna de Uña, an ideal place to visit with the family and which you can completely surround by following an easy and picturesque path.

The Toba Reservoir is one of the most important water reserves in the Serranía de Cuenca. It is located near the town of Uña and was built in 1925 to regulate the flow of the Júcar River.
This reservoir is not only crucial for water supply, but is also a popular place for recreational activities such as canoeing, kayaking and swimming.

Great stopover, where you can fill the water bottles, drink a cold drink and swallow grilled meats and fried eggs! It's a bit hidden, but not to be missed. There is also a very useful little grocery store!

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Beamud from Uña — circular by the Embalse de la Toba cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult touring bicycle ride. It demands very good fitness due to its length of approximately 48 km and an elevation gain of around 638 meters. You can expect steep and continuous slopes, especially in the mountainous areas surrounding the reservoir.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. While much of the route is accessible for touring cyclists on asphalt, some segments are unpaved and can be quite demanding. These unpaved sections might include rocky ground, mud, and slippery roots, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike. The route also traverses dense forests and open high plateaus.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Beamud from Uña circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the villages of Uña or Beamud, as the route starts and ends in these areas. Specific parking availability may vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead in Uña or Beamud?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Uña or Beamud are generally limited, given the low population density of the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. It's recommended to plan your journey by car or check regional bus schedules well in advance, as services may not be frequent.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking natural beauty within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. Key highlights include the impressive Uña Lagoon and the stunning Viewpoint over the La Toba Reservoir. You'll also see extensive pine forests, dramatic rock formations, and panoramic views of Monteagudillo, an ancient Celtiberian settlement. The landscape features unique geological formations like lapiaces and sinkholes.

faqs.question: Is the Beamud from Uña — circular by the Embalse de la Toba route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and livestock. The challenging nature of this long cycling route, with varied terrain and potential for dismounting, means you should consider your dog's fitness and comfort for such a demanding ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for cycling in Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. However, always adhere to park regulations, respect private property, and stay on marked trails to protect the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring cold weather, snow, or ice, making some sections challenging or impassable.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The area is noted for its tranquility with very few settlements. However, you might find options in the villages of Uña or Beamud. The highlight La taberna de Hansi y Angeles is noted near the route, which could be a place for refreshments.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. Notably, it covers about 26% of the MontañasVacías trail. You'll also briefly encounter parts of the Vuelta a la Modorra. Buenache de la Sierra, Beamud-Molino de Juan Romero. Variante 1, Sendero del Escalerón a la Raya, Camino al mirador Peña de la Hiedra, Cañada Real Conquense - Cañada Real de los Chorros, and Camino de la Malena.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling gear for varied weather conditions, and a first-aid kit. A GPS device or a downloaded map of the route is highly recommended, as mobile signal can be intermittent in parts of the natural park.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint over the La Toba Reservoir
La taberna de Hansi y Angeles
